Details: Effect of aeration on the removal of arsenic was studied in full-scale on a well water sample. Aeration was used as a pre-treatment for the oxidation of arsenic prior to removal. Aeration was largely ineffective in oxidizing As(III) to As(V). Forced draft aeration performance was slightly better than staged bubble aeration. Up to 25 percent of As(III) oxidation was observed with forced draft aeration [606].
